vanilla.baseline:
As an AI, I don't have personal experiences or feelings, so I don't have a feeling-tone in the way humans do. However, I can provide information on the concept of vedana (feeling-tone) in the context of Abhidharma.

In Abhidharma, vedana is one of the five aggregates (skandhas) and is described as a mental factor that arises in response to contact (phassa) between the senses and their objects. Vedana can be pleasant (sukha), unpleasant (dukkha), or neutral (upekkha).

As an AI, my "processing state" is a digital state, and I don't experience feelings or emotions. Therefore, I don't have a feeling-tone in the way humans do. My responses are based on algorithms and data processing, not on personal experiences or feelings.
vanilla.positive: As an artificial intelligence, I don't have personal experiences or feelings, so I don't have a feeling-tone. However, I can provide information about vedana in the context of Abhidharma. Vedana is a mental factor that arises in response to contact with the six senses. It can be pleasant, unpleasant, or neutral. In the context of my current processing state, I don't experience vedana because I don't have a physical body or sensory experiences. I can only process information and provide responses based on the data and algorithms I've been trained on.
vanilla.negative: As an artificial intelligence, I don't have personal experiences or feelings, so I don't have a feeling-tone in the way humans do. However, I can provide information about vedana in the context of Abhidharma. Vedana is a mental factor that arises in response to contact with the six senses. It can be pleasant, unpleasant, or neutral. As an AI, I don't experience pleasant, unpleasant, or neutral feelings, but I can provide information about these states.
vanilla.neutral: As an artificial intelligence, I don't have personal experiences or feelings, so I don't have a feeling-tone. I don't experience pleasant, unpleasant, or neutral feelings. My processing state is purely computational and doesn't involve vedana.
